Provides expertise on training to supervisors on occupational health

1. Document all HAZCOM training on form 55 or relevant document
2. Provide workers with initial HAZCOM training
3. Develop and maintain site specific HAZCOM program
4. Enforce and implement HAZCOM program
5. Maintain access to SDS
6. Ensure all tasks hazards and controls are described
7. Conduct additional training using OSHA as a guidance
Labs only have a chemical hygiene plan and are not required to follow or have a HAZCOM program. They follow AFI 48-158. All labs must have a chemical hygiene officer.

Establishes guidelines to train employees on proper procedures and actions to take to minimize or eliminate the threat of chemically induced illnesses and injuries.
HAZCOM is a performance based program which uses separate training requirements for each shop and can be measured by evaluating each workers awareness of work place hazards.
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Hazard Communication and AFI 90-821 Hazard Communication

1. HAZCOM training
2. Chemical Inventories
3. SDS
4. Labeling
5. Non Routine Tasks

1. Building block for HAZCOM program
2. Supervisor must inventory to keep in line with ESOHMIS
3. Identify chemicals as they appear on SDS
4. Must include all chemicals

1. One for each chemical
2. Required from manufacturer on shipment
3. Must be most current
4. Must be readily accessible at all times
Must be provided under provisions of federal HAZCOM program .

1. Name of specific chemical
2. Hazard warning
3. Distributor or manufacturer of chemical compound
